pattern: One pass tracking the previous value and the index of every local max/min found so far turns the min and max gap between critical points into a running comparison, with no need to store the whole list.
hints: A node can only be a local max or min if it has both a previous and a next node, so the first and last nodes never count.

Walk the list once keeping the previous value; whenever the current node is a strict local max or min, compare its index against the last critical index seen to update the running minimum gap, and remember the very first critical index for the final maximum gap.
If fewer than two critical points ever show up, the answer is [-1, -1]; otherwise the maximum gap is simply the last critical index minus the first.

edge_inputs.description: too short to contain an interior node at all
strictly monotonic list, no local extremum exists
smallest list that can contain two critical points
all equal values, strict inequality never satisfied
zigzag producing several critical points so min and max gaps differ
plateau touching both neighbors, equal values must not count as extrema
exactly one interior critical point, which alone is not enough

stress.description: random values across the max list length
